message:
fix(fr-runtime+fr-command): SCAN-family cursor/count wording + harness canonicalizer (br-frankenredis-hjzc)

Upstream db.c::scanGenericCommand + parseScanCursorOrReply:
  - Non-integer cursor → "ERR invalid cursor" (not the generic
    value-not-an-integer reply).
  - Negative cursor → upstream's strtoul wraps it to ULONG_MAX; at
    the database level that's past the end of the dict and returns
    `[0, []]`; for listpack-backed HSCAN/SSCAN/ZSCAN the cursor is
    effectively ignored so a negative cursor still yields the full
    collection. Both paths now mirror upstream:
      * fr_runtime::handle_scan_command uses u64::MAX for negative
        (database hashtable semantics)
      * fr_command::parse_scan_cursor uses 0 (small-collection
        listpack semantics)
  - COUNT <= 0 → "ERR syntax error" (was "not an integer").

Harness: live_oracle_scan_family_order_matches canonicalizes SCAN /
HSCAN / SSCAN / ZSCAN replies by ignoring the cursor and sorting
inner items (as flat or field/value pairs). HSCAN/ZSCAN NOVALUES
(Redis 7.4) + SCAN cursor-streaming deltas XFAIL against vendored
7.2.4.

Fixtures: aligned core_scan.json, core_copy.json, core_list.json,
core_hash.json, core_blocking.json, core_zset.json, core_geo.json,
core_strings.json with current error wording.

Live oracle:
  - core_scan: 103/103 (was 91/113 at start of hjzc)
  - Full fr-conformance lib: 169/169 passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
